title: Hippeastrum morrisclintii
taxonomy: Hippeastrum morrisclintii is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, native to South America. It is a member of the Hippeastrum genus, which is commonly known as the Amaryllis. The plant is known for its large, showy flowers and is often cultivated for its ornamental value. As a member of the Amaryllidaceae family, Hippeastrum morrisclintii is closely related to other flowering plants such as Galtonia and Crinum. The exact classification of Hippeastrum morrisclintii may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted to be a species within the Hippeastrum genus.
